key_facts: Boats under 15m (like Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) can be rented without a license in Spain.
Our Tier A boats cost €749/2h or €2,299/8h, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and VAT.
Mangusta 80 (24m) requires a license – but our Tier B price includes a free jet ski.
All charters include a licensed skipper, so you never need a license yourself.

faq.q: Do I need a license to rent a boat in Marbella?
faq.a: No. All our charters include a licensed skipper who handles navigation. You can relax and enjoy the coast without any paperwork. This applies to every boat in our fleet, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 to the 29m Ferretti 94.

faq.q: What is the cheapest boat rental without a license in Marbella?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats – the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 – start at €749 for 2 hours. Both are 12.5m, seat up to 11 guests, and include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. For a full day (8 hours), the price is €2,299.

faq.q: Can I rent a larger boat like the Mangusta 80 without a license?
faq.a: Yes, you can, because a licensed skipper is always included. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) costs €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours, and that price includes a free jet ski. You don’t need any license to charter it.

faq.q: What is included in the boat rental price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden fees – the price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: How do I book a boat without a license in Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for boats with pricing on request. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer, to secure your preferred date and boat.

faq.q: What if I want to rent a boat for a longer period?
faq.a: For Tier A boats, the 8-hour rate is €2,299. For other boats like the Mangusta 80, we offer custom durations – just ask via WhatsApp. Longer charters often include lunch stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés.

faq.q: Are there any additional costs I should expect?
faq.a: No. The price covers everything listed above. Optional extras like a private chef, premium champagne, or water toys can be arranged at an extra cost, but we’ll always confirm these before booking.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of drinks and snacks, but you can supplement it with your own favourites. Just let us know if you need a cooler.

data.body_html:
Thinking about a boat rental no license Marbella cost for 2026? You’re in the right place. Marbella’s coastline – from Puerto Banús to Estepona – is best explored by boat, and you don’t need a single piece of paper to charter one. Every boat in our fleet comes with a licensed skipper, so you can focus on the sun, sea, and views of La Concha. Below is a full, transparent breakdown of what you’ll pay, what’s included, and which boats are available.

Why You Don’t Need a License

Spanish maritime law requires a license for boats over 15m or for private use without a skipper. But on our charters, a professional skipper is always included in the price. This means you can charter any boat – from a 6.4m speedboat to a 29m superyacht – without holding any qualification. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety, leaving you to enjoy the Mediterranean.

Transparent Pricing: Tier A Boats

Our most popular no-license option is the Tier A category, which includes the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39. Both are 12.5m, seat up to 11 passengers, and depart from Puerto Banús. Prices are fixed and all-inclusive:

  • 2 hours: €749
  • 4 hours: from €1,299
  • 8 hours (full day): €2,299

These prices include the skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and 21% IVA. No surprises.

Tier B: Mangusta 80 (with Free Jet Ski)

If you want more space and a touch of luxury, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is available from €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours. This price includes a free jet ski – perfect for exploring coves like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés. A skipper is included, so no license needed. The same applies to the white and grey variants.

Other Boats: Pricing on Request

For larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 (29m), Canados 86 (26m), or Maiora 26m, pricing is custom. Contact us via WhatsApp for a quote. All include a skipper, so you never need a license. Typical itineraries include stops at Cabopino, Estepona, or Sotogrande.

What’s Included in Every Charter

Every boat rental includes:

  • Licensed skipper
  • Fuel for the full charter duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

There are no hidden fees. The price you see is the price you pay.

Which Boats Can You Rent Without a License?

All of them. Because a skipper is always included, you can charter any boat in our fleet without a license. Here’s a quick overview of popular options:

BoatLengthMax PaxStarting Price
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h
Speedboat7m6On request
Ferretti 9429m12On request

How to Book

Ready to reserve? Visit our boat rental Marbella hub to browse availability. For Tier A boats, you can book directly online. For others, send us a WhatsApp message – we’ll confirm pricing and availability within hours. We recommend booking at least 48 hours ahead, especially in July and August.

Frequently asked questions

Do I need a license to rent a boat in Marbella?

No. All our charters include a licensed skipper who handles navigation. You can relax and enjoy the coast without any paperwork. This applies to every boat in our fleet, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 to the 29m Ferretti 94.

What is the cheapest boat rental without a license in Marbella?

Our Tier A boats – the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 – start at €749 for 2 hours. Both are 12.5m, seat up to 11 guests, and include skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. For a full day (8 hours), the price is €2,299.

Can I rent a larger boat like the Mangusta 80 without a license?

Yes, you can, because a licensed skipper is always included. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) costs €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours, and that price includes a free jet ski. You don’t need any license to charter it.

What is included in the boat rental price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden fees – the price you see is the price you pay.

How do I book a boat without a license in Marbella?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for boats with pricing on request. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during summer, to secure your preferred date and boat.

What if I want to rent a boat for a longer period?

For Tier A boats, the 8-hour rate is €2,299. For other boats like the Mangusta 80, we offer custom durations – just ask via WhatsApp. Longer charters often include lunch stops at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés.

Are there any additional costs I should expect?

No. The price covers everything listed above. Optional extras like a private chef, premium champagne, or water toys can be arranged at an extra cost, but we’ll always confirm these before booking.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of drinks and snacks, but you can supplement it with your own favourites. Just let us know if you need a cooler.
